ssrIdeaData.name: Traders_Reality_Lib
ssrIdeaData.description:
Library  [b]"Traders_Reality_Lib"[/b]
This library contains common elements used in Traders Reality scripts

[b]calcPvsra(pvsraVolume, pvsraHigh, pvsraLow, pvsraClose, pvsraOpen, redVectorColor, greenVectorColor, violetVectorColor, blueVectorColor, darkGreyCandleColor, lightGrayCandleColor)[/b]
  calculate the pvsra candle color and return the color as well as an alert if a vector candle has apperared.
Situation "Climax"
Bars with volume >= 200% of the average volume of the 10 previous chart TFs, or bars
where the product of candle spread x candle volume is >= the highest for the 10 previous
chart time TFs.
Default Colors:  Bull bars are green and bear bars are red.
Situation "Volume Rising Above Average"
Bars with volume >= 150% of the average volume of the 10 previous chart TFs.
Default Colors:  Bull bars are blue and bear are violet.
  Parameters:
    [b]pvsraVolume[/b]: the instrument volume series (obtained from request.sequrity)
    [b]pvsraHigh[/b]: the instrument high series (obtained from request.sequrity)
    [b]pvsraLow[/b]: the instrument low series (obtained from request.sequrity)
    [b]pvsraClose[/b]: the instrument close series (obtained from request.sequrity)
    [b]pvsraOpen[/b]: the instrument open series (obtained from request.sequrity)
    [b]redVectorColor[/b]: red vector candle color
    [b]greenVectorColor[/b]: green vector candle color
    [b]violetVectorColor[/b]: violet/pink vector candle color
    [b]blueVectorColor[/b]: blue vector candle color
    [b]darkGreyCandleColor[/b]: regular volume candle down candle color - not a vector 
    [b]lightGrayCandleColor[/b]: regular volume candle up candle color - not a vector
@return [pvsraColor, alertFlag, averageVolume, volumeSpread, highestVolumeSpread]

[b]adr(length, barsBack)[/b]
  Parameters:
    [b]length[/b]: how many elements of the series to calculate on
    [b]barsBack[/b]: starting possition for the length calculation - current bar or some other value eg last bar
@return adr the adr for the specified lenght

[b]adrHigh(adr, fromDo)[/b]
  Calculate the ADR high given an ADR
  Parameters:
    [b]adr[/b]: the adr
    [b]fromDo[/b]: boolean flag, if false calculate traditional adr from high low of today, if true calcualte from exchange midnight
@return adrHigh the position of the adr high in price

[b]adrLow(adr, fromDo)[/b]
  Parameters:
    [b]adr[/b]: the adr
    [b]fromDo[/b]: boolean flag, if false calculate traditional adr from high low of today, if true calcualte from exchange midnight
@return adrLow the position of the adr low in price

[b]splitSessionString(sessXTime)[/b]
  given a session in the format 0000-0100:23456 split out the hours and minutes
  Parameters:
    [b]sessXTime[/b]: the session time string usually in the format 0000-0100:23456
@return [startHour, startMin, endHour, endMin]

[b]calcSessionStartEnd(sessXTime, gmt)[/b]
  calculate the start and end timestamps of the session
  Parameters:
    [b]sessXTime[/b]: the session time string usually in the format 0000-0100:23456 
    [b]gmt[/b]: the gmt offset string usually in the format GMT+1 or GMT+2 etc
@return [targetstartTimeX,targetendTimeX]

[b]drawOpenRange(sessXTime, sessXcol, showOrX, gmt)[/b]
  draw open range for a session
  Parameters:
    [b]sessXTime[/b]: session string in the format 0000-0100:23456 
    [b]sessXcol[/b]: the color to be used for the opening range box shading
    [b]showOrX[/b]: boolean flag to toggle displaying the opening range
    [b]gmt[/b]: the gmt offset string usually in the format GMT+1 or GMT+2 etc
@return void

[b]drawSessionHiLo(sessXTime, show_rectangleX, show_labelX, sessXcolLabel, sessXLabel, gmt, sessionLineStyle)[/b]
  Parameters:
    [b]sessXTime[/b]: session string in the format 0000-0100:23456 
    [b]show_rectangleX[/b]: show the session high and low lines 
    [b]show_labelX[/b]: show the session label
    [b]sessXcolLabel[/b]: the color to be used for the hi/low lines and label
    [b]sessXLabel[/b]: the session label text
    [b]gmt[/b]: the gmt offset string usually in the format GMT+1 or GMT+2 etc
    [b]sessionLineStyle[/b]: the line stile for the session high low lines 
@return void

[b]calcDst()[/b]
  calculate market session dst on/off flags 
@return [nyDST, ukDST, sydDST] indicating if DST is on or off for a particular region

[b]timestampPreviousDayOfWeek(previousDayOfWeek, hourOfDay, gmtOffset, oneWeekMillis)[/b]
  Timestamp any of the 6 previous days in the week (such as last Wednesday at 21 hours GMT)
  Parameters:
    [b]previousDayOfWeek[/b]: Monday or Satruday
    [b]hourOfDay[/b]: the hour of the day when psy calc is to start
    [b]gmtOffset[/b]: the gmt offset string usually in the format GMT+1 or GMT+2 etc
    [b]oneWeekMillis[/b]: the amount if time for a week in milliseconds
@return the timestamp of the psy level calculation start time

[b]getdayOpen()[/b]
  get the daily open - basically exchange midnight
@return the daily open value which is float price

[b]newBar(res)[/b]
  new_bar: check if we're on a new bar within the session in a given resolution
  Parameters:
    [b]res[/b]: the desired resolution
@return true/false is a new bar for the session has started

[b]toPips(val)[/b]
  to_pips Convert value to pips
  Parameters:
    [b]val[/b]: the value to convert to pips
@return the value in pips

[b]rLabel(ry, rtext, rstyle, rcolor, valid, labelXOffset)[/b]
  a function that draws a right aligned lable for a series during the current bar
  Parameters:
    [b]ry[/b]: series float the y coordinate of the lable
    [b]rtext[/b]: the text of the label
    [b]rstyle[/b]: the style for the lable
    [b]rcolor[/b]: the color for the label
    [b]valid[/b]: a boolean flag that allows for turning on or off a lable
    [b]labelXOffset[/b]: how much to offset the label from the current position

[b]rLabelOffset(ry, rtext, rstyle, rcolor, valid, labelXOffset)[/b]
  a function that draws a right aligned lable for a series during the current bar
  Parameters:
    [b]ry[/b]: series float the y coordinate of the lable
    [b]rtext[/b]: the text of the label
    [b]rstyle[/b]: the style for the lable
    [b]rcolor[/b]: the color for the label
    [b]valid[/b]: a boolean flag that allows for turning on or off a lable
    [b]labelXOffset[/b]: how much to offset the label from the current position

[b]rLabelLastBar(ry, rtext, rstyle, rcolor, valid, labelXOffset)[/b]
  a function that draws a right aligned lable for a series only on the last bar
  Parameters:
    [b]ry[/b]: series float the y coordinate of the lable
    [b]rtext[/b]: the text of the label
    [b]rstyle[/b]: the style for the lable
    [b]rcolor[/b]: the color for the label
    [b]valid[/b]: a boolean flag that allows for turning on or off a lable
    [b]labelXOffset[/b]: how much to offset the label from the current position

[b]drawLine(xSeries, res, tag, xColor, xStyle, xWidth, xExtend, isLabelValid, labelXOffset, validTimeFrame)[/b]
  a function that draws a line and a label for a series
  Parameters:
    [b]xSeries[/b]: series float the y coordinate of the line/label
    [b]res[/b]: the desired resolution controlling when a new line will start
    [b]tag[/b]: the text for the lable
    [b]xColor[/b]: the color for the label
    [b]xStyle[/b]: the style for the line
    [b]xWidth[/b]: the width of the line
    [b]xExtend[/b]: extend the line
    [b]isLabelValid[/b]: a boolean flag that allows for turning on or off a label
    [b]labelXOffset[/b]: how much to offset the label from the current position
    [b]validTimeFrame[/b]: a boolean flag that allows for turning on or off a line drawn

[b]drawLineDO(xSeries, res, tag, xColor, xStyle, xWidth, xExtend, isLabelValid, labelXOffset, validTimeFrame)[/b]
  a function that draws a line and a label for the daily open series
  Parameters:
    [b]xSeries[/b]: series float the y coordinate of the line/label
    [b]res[/b]: the desired resolution controlling when a new line will start
    [b]tag[/b]: the text for the lable
    [b]xColor[/b]: the color for the label
    [b]xStyle[/b]: the style for the line
    [b]xWidth[/b]: the width of the line
    [b]xExtend[/b]: extend the line
    [b]isLabelValid[/b]: a boolean flag that allows for turning on or off a label
    [b]labelXOffset[/b]: how much to offset the label from the current position
    [b]validTimeFrame[/b]: a boolean flag that allows for turning on or off a line drawn

[b]drawPivot(pivotLevel, res, tag, pivotColor, pivotLabelColor, pivotStyle, pivotWidth, pivotExtend, isLabelValid, validTimeFrame, levelStart, pivotLabelXOffset)[/b]
  draw a pivot line - the line starts one day into the past
  Parameters:
    [b]pivotLevel[/b]: series of the pivot point
    [b]res[/b]: the desired resolution
    [b]tag[/b]: the text to appear
    [b]pivotColor[/b]: the color of the line
    [b]pivotLabelColor[/b]: the color of the label
    [b]pivotStyle[/b]: the line style
    [b]pivotWidth[/b]: the line width
    [b]pivotExtend[/b]: extend the line
    [b]isLabelValid[/b]: boolean param allows to turn label on and off
    [b]validTimeFrame[/b]: only draw the line and label at a valid timeframe
    [b]levelStart[/b]: basically when to start drawing the levels
    [b]pivotLabelXOffset[/b]: how much to offset the label from its current postion
@return the pivot line series

[b]getPvsraFlagByColor(pvsraColor, redVectorColor, greenVectorColor, violetVectorColor, blueVectorColor, lightGrayCandleColor)[/b]
  convert the pvsra color to an internal code
  Parameters:
    [b]pvsraColor[/b]: the calculated pvsra color
    [b]redVectorColor[/b]: the user defined red vector color
    [b]greenVectorColor[/b]: the user defined green vector color
    [b]violetVectorColor[/b]: the user defined violet vector color
    [b]blueVectorColor[/b]: the user defined blue vector color
    [b]lightGrayCandleColor[/b]: the user defined regular up candle color
@return pvsra internal code

[b]updateZones(pvsra, direction, boxArr, maxlevels, pvsraHigh, pvsraLow, pvsraOpen, pvsraClose, transperancy, zoneupdatetype, zonecolor, zonetype, borderwidth, coloroverride, redVectorColor, greenVectorColor, violetVectorColor, blueVectorColor, lightGrayCandleColor)[/b]
  a function that draws the unrecovered vector candle zones
  Parameters:
    [b]pvsra[/b]: internal code
    [b]direction[/b]: above or below the current pa
    [b]boxArr[/b]: the array containing the boxes that need to be updated
    [b]maxlevels[/b]: the maximum number of boxes to draw
    [b]pvsraHigh[/b]: the pvsra high value series
    [b]pvsraLow[/b]: the pvsra low value series
    [b]pvsraOpen[/b]: the pvsra open value series
    [b]pvsraClose[/b]: the pvsra close value series
    [b]transperancy[/b]: the transparencfy of the vecor candle zones
    [b]zoneupdatetype[/b]: the zone update type
    [b]zonecolor[/b]: the zone color if overriden
    [b]zonetype[/b]: the zone type
    [b]borderwidth[/b]: the width of the border
    [b]coloroverride[/b]: if the color overriden
    [b]redVectorColor[/b]: the user defined red vector color
    [b]greenVectorColor[/b]: the user defined green vector color
    [b]violetVectorColor[/b]: the user defined violet vector color
    [b]blueVectorColor[/b]: the user defined blue vector color
    [b]lightGrayCandleColor[/b]: the user defined regular up candle color

[b]cleanarr(arr)[/b]
  clean an array from na values
  Parameters:
    [b]arr[/b]: the array to clean
@return if the array was cleaned

[b]calcPsyLevels(oneWeekMillis, showPsylevels, psyType, sydDST)[/b]
  calculate the psy levels
4 hour res based on how mt4 does it
mt4 code
int Li_4 = iBarShift(NULL, PERIOD_H4, iTime(NULL, PERIOD_W1, Li_0)) - 2 - Offset;
ObjectCreate("PsychHi", OBJ_TREND, 0, Time[0], iHigh(NULL, PERIOD_H4, iHighest(NULL, PERIOD_H4, MODE_HIGH, 2, Li_4)), iTime(NULL, PERIOD_W1, 0), iHigh(NULL, PERIOD_H4,
iHighest(NULL, PERIOD_H4, MODE_HIGH, 2, Li_4)));
so basically because the session is 8 hours and we are looking at a 4 hour resolution we only need to take the highest high an lowest low of 2 bars
we use the gmt offset to adjust the 0000-0800 session to Sydney open which is at 2100 during dst and at 2200 otherwize. (dst - spring foward, fall back)
keep in mind sydney is in the souther hemisphere so dst is oposite of when london and new york go into dst
  Parameters:
    [b]oneWeekMillis[/b]: a constant value
    [b]showPsylevels[/b]: should psy levels be calculated
    [b]psyType[/b]: the type of Psylevels - crypto or forex
    [b]sydDST[/b]: is Sydney in DST
@return [psyHi, psyLo, psyHiLabel, psyLoLabel, psySessionStartTime]

children: This library contains common elements used in Traders Reality scripts
calcPvsra(pvsraVolume, pvsraHigh, pvsraLow, pvsraClose, pvsraOpen, redVectorColor, greenVectorColor, violetVectorColor, blueVectorColor, darkGreyCandleColor, lightGrayCandleColor)

calculate the pvsra candle color and return the color as well as an alert if a vector candle has apperared.
Situation "Climax"
Bars with volume >= 200% of the average volume of the 10 previous chart TFs, or bars
where the product of candle spread x candle volume is >= the highest for the 10 previous
chart time TFs.
Default Colors:  Bull bars are green and bear bars are red.
Situation "Volume Rising Above Average"
Bars with volume >= 150% of the average volume of the 10 previous chart TFs.
Default Colors:  Bull bars are blue and bear are violet.
  Parameters:
: the instrument volume series (obtained from request.sequrity)
: the instrument high series (obtained from request.sequrity)
: the instrument low series (obtained from request.sequrity)
: the instrument close series (obtained from request.sequrity)
: the instrument open series (obtained from request.sequrity)
: regular volume candle down candle color - not a vector
: regular volume candle up candle color - not a vector
: how many elements of the series to calculate on
: starting possition for the length calculation - current bar or some other value eg last bar
adr the adr for the specified lenght

: boolean flag, if false calculate traditional adr from high low of today, if true calcualte from exchange midnight
adrHigh the position of the adr high in price

updates.description:
v2

Added:
[b]adrHiLo(length, barsBack, fromDO)[/b]
  Parameters:
    [b]length[/b]: how many elements of the series to calculate on
    [b]barsBack[/b]: starting possition for the length calculation - current bar or some other value eg last bar
    [b]fromDO[/b]: boolean flag, if false calculate traditional adr from high low of today, if true calcualte from exchange midnight
@return adr, adrLow and adrHigh  - the adr, the position of the adr High and adr Low with respect to price

[b]drawSessionHiloLite(sessXTime, show_rectangleX, show_labelX, sessXcolLabel, sessXLabel, gmt, sessionLineStyle, sessXcol)[/b]
  Parameters:
    [b]sessXTime[/b]: session string in the format 0000-0100:23456 
    [b]show_rectangleX[/b]: show the session high and low lines 
    [b]show_labelX[/b]: show the session label
    [b]sessXcolLabel[/b]: the color to be used for the hi/low lines and label
    [b]sessXLabel[/b]: the session label text
    [b]gmt[/b]: the gmt offset string usually in the format GMT+1 or GMT+2 etc
    [b]sessionLineStyle[/b]: the line stile for the session high low lines 
    [b]sessXcol[/b]: - the color for the box color that will color the session
@return void

[b]msToHmsString(ms)[/b]
  converts milliseconds into an hh:mm string. For example, 61000 ms to '0:01:01'
  Parameters:
    [b]ms[/b]: - the milliseconds to convert to hh:mm
@return string - the converted hh:mm string

[b]countdownString(openToday, closeToday)[/b]
  that calculates how much time is left until the next session taking the session start and end times into account. Note this function does not work on intraday sessions.
  Parameters:
    [b]openToday[/b]: - timestamps of when the session opens in general - note its a series because the timestamp was created using the dst flag which is a series itself thus producing a timestamp series
    [b]closeToday[/b]: - timestamp of when the session closes in general - note its a series because the timestamp was created using the dst flag which is a series itself thus producing a timestamp series
@return a countdown of when next the session opens or 'Open' if the session is open now

[b]countdownStringSyd(openToday, closeToday)[/b]
  that calculates how much time is left until the next session taking the session start and end times into account. special case of intraday sessions like sydney
  Parameters:
    [b]openToday[/b]: - timestamps of when the session opens in general - note its a series because the timestamp was created using the dst flag which is a series itself thus producing a timestamp series
    [b]closeToday[/b]: - timestamp of when the session closes in general - note its a series because the timestamp was created using the dst flag which is a series itself thus producing a timestamp series
@return a countdown of when next the session opens or 'Open' if the session is open now

updates.label: Release Notes
